title: Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory
review:

title: Final Destination
review:
really fun and a different take on horror when you use fears and suspense rather than jump scares and demons. death is my largest fear and this did a wonderful job showing how inevitable it is and how terrifyingly it can come at any time. the death scenes were silly fun and i am a big fan of the sentient goo

Last Watched: January 4th, 2026

title: Interstellar
review:
god this movie is so gutting and beautiful. the film itself is very visually stunning and unique, but the story is just absolutely perfect and heartbreaking. i knew nothing about the movie going into it, so just finding out he was the ghost, and how he even told himself to stay is so devastating. the scene where he finds out his children have aged 20 years is just shot and acted perfectly. and the starting video being about cooper but being told by his daughter after the fact, it was all perfection and perfectly put together. i love stories about time, and this one might be my favorite

Last Watched: April 28th, 2026

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
review:
this movie is so beautiful and fun. not many movies take advantage of the multiverse concept that aren't superhero films, so to see one be done so well and unique is amazing. its just such a fun idea of doing random things to get to a new multiverse for essentially a stat increase. me personally im jumping to every possible multiverse and leveling myself up, even if i gotta jump on the plug. i love the unique multiverses too, the rock one and the pinata are just a masterclass. and the cat universe of course, i just love unique displays of infinite possibilities. the title cards, music, acting, and visuals are just off the charts. the action scenes are done so well with the slow motion and where the fights go. i absolutely love the message, of both life and generational trauma. life is meaningless, but why does that have to make it a bad thing? if nothing matters, EVERYTHING matters.

Last Watched: March 17th, 2026

title: The Substance
review:
i truly and honestly don’t even know what to say. this film isn’t subtle with its messages, but my god does it go to lengths to convey it. the noise, atmosphere, and camera work do a REMARKABLE job making you feel uncomfortable and uneasy all-throughout, and the ending genuinely shocked me and left me baffled. took me entirely off guard. don’t watch while eating or in public though 🙂👍

Last Watched: October 4th, 2025

title: Sinners
review:
was absolutely blown away by the music and cinematography in this film. the story was phenomenal, and it caught me off guard how fast we were thrown into this story and how no punches were pulled. i like the random ensemble that was fighting, i think they blended well and had great chemistry together. i wish to see prequels of the twins past, and the evident sequel sounds exciting. not to mention the wonderful acting on everyone, it goes without saying.

title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
review:
incredibly fun, haters need more whimsey and to not take things so serious. i see where their arguments come from but grow up and just let yourself enjoy silliness. great shine for ninji, wish some things went unannounced before the film though because it’d have been so much sicker!!!!! next movie needs even more ninji

Last Watched: April 2nd, 2026

title: Send Help
review:
knew nothing about the film other than raimi directed it, was not disappointed whatsoever. i really love an unreliable narrator story, and i was fully on linda's side because every billionaire should be abolished, and the slow turn and build up of her doing stuff more and more unredeemable was class. i did not see the end coming of her actually having a place, which caught me off guard and made me realize the whole time what i was watching wasn't the story that actually happened. made me want to rewatch immediately to see parts i missed. very fun film 

Last Watched: May 17th, 2026
